name: Texas Funding Corporation
description_short: Family-owned hard money lender in Houston since 1982, specializing in collateral-based loans for real estate investors and commercial borrowers who need fast funding.
description_long:
Texas Funding Corporation was founded in 1982 and has operated as a private money lender for over 35 years in the Houston area. The company is family-run and led by President J. Glenn Lee, who brings extensive real estate industry experience. Based in Houston, TX, the firm operates throughout Texas and the Southwest, with a regional focus that allows them to understand local markets deeply. They have lent millions of dollars over their operational history.

Texas Funding specializes in hard money loans and private money lending secured by collateral, primarily real estate. Their core offerings include hard money loans for commercial real estate investors, land investors, and wholesale land buyers. They also originate mortgage notes and purchase performing, sub-performing, and non-performing real estate mortgage notes. Their target clients are businesses and investors seeking liquidity and fast funding solutions, rather than traditional consumers seeking personal loans.

The company distinguishes itself through speed of funding and willingness to work with borrowers who may have poor personal credit or non-traditional financial profiles. They operate as a direct lender without brokers, which they claim enables faster closings—often in days rather than weeks or months. Their approach emphasizes minimal paperwork, no pre-qualification requirements, and collateral-based underwriting rather than credit-score-dependent approval processes.

Texas Funding operates in a legitimate but specialized niche of real estate financing. Borrowers should understand that hard money loans typically carry higher interest rates and shorter terms than traditional mortgages, and are designed for short-term bridge financing rather than long-term home mortgages. The company's focus on commercial real estate and investor clients makes them unsuitable for typical residential mortgage needs.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Established 1982 family business with 35+ years of hard money lending experience and millions lent
Direct lender model eliminates broker involvement, enabling faster closings (stated as days vs. weeks/months)
Minimal paperwork and no pre-qualification process reduces application friction
Willing to fund borrowers with poor personal credit if collateral is sufficient
Regional market expertise in Texas and Southwest allows informed underwriting decisions
Services include hard money loans, mortgage note purchases, and real estate investment options
President J. Glenn Lee has 35+ years real estate experience with experienced staff backing loan decisions
cons: Hard money loans typically carry significantly higher interest rates than traditional mortgages—rates not disclosed on website
Short-term loan structure designed for investors/flippers, not primary residence or long-term financing
Limited transparency on loan terms, fees, APR ranges, and approval timeline specifics
Collateral-based lending means personal assets are at risk if business venture fails
Regional focus (Texas/Southwest) may limit availability outside their service area
best_for: Commercial real estate investors needing quick bridge financing for acquisitions
Land investors and wholesale buyers requiring fast closing to secure opportunities
Borrowers with poor credit who have valuable real estate collateral
Businesses and institutions seeking liquidity through mortgage note purchases
diagnosis: Texas Funding is best suited for real estate investors, land buyers, and commercial borrowers who need rapid access to capital and have valuable collateral, but understand hard money loans carry higher costs and shorter terms than traditional financing. Residential mortgage borrowers should seek traditional lenders instead.
services: Hard money loans for real estate investment
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Family-owned hard money lender in Houston since 1982 specializing in collateral-based loans for real estate investors and commercial borrowers.
